relations.note: The color pairing rules provide concrete, brand-specific examples of allowed and forbidden combinations that map directly to the general contrast standard.

quotes: Test contrast levels (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair. [GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]

rules.intent: Ensure visual accessibility and WCAG compliance across all text presentations.
rules.rule_text: Every text/background pair must meet minimum WCAG AA contrast standards: at least 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text (defined as >=24px or 19px bold). Colors must be verified using tools like webaim.com.

quotes: Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Maintain brand visual legibility by enforcing verified color pairings and restricting low-contrast text colors.
rules.rule_text: Do not pair Gold (color.primary.gold = #ffc60a) or Sunshine (color.primary.sunshine = #faa31b) text on White backgrounds as it fails contrast. Golds must be restricted to surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis. Known-safe pairings include Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, or Graphite on White, Light Blue, or Golden Sand; and White on Brand Blue, Deep Blue, or Dark Navy.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Support colorblind and visually impaired users by offering redundant visual/contextual signifiers.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely solely on color to communicate important information or meaning; color cues must be accompanied by text labels, icons, depth, clear copy, or other visual identifiers.
